transcript.text: Living in Lafayette, V-Log, the 6th of May, 2013.
Today was a stony day. I think the last of it, though.
Got up, drove into town to move furniture for my mom with Mike.
But I got in town and then I realized I forgot all my electronical stuff,
So I left him off and came back and then got my GPS bike, computer,
And a helmet cam and this camera.
And then went in and worked for a couple hours, moving stuff out.
And then Mike went to hang out with his parents.
So I drove up all the way to Potos, I preserved to do my first mountain bike ride,
But I couldn't find my helmet cam.
So I drove all the way back down to the bike shop again and they didn't have it.
So then I looked in the van again and I was under the seat, the points to the back.
And I think I might want to take those seats out and take up a lot of space.
So tomorrow I'm going to come up with a system for packing the car and packing all my stuff.
I haven't gotten around to that yet.
And I did the bike computer, though.
This is the ride that I did.
This is the ride that I rode for an hour 55 and I went 42, uh, uh, uh, huh, it's over here.
I did 24.1 kilometers with 800 meters of elevation gain.
And I burned 1,000 kilometers, so that's pretty cool.
Um, I did go down and then noticed that my, uh, my right cleat was loose.
So I came back and tightened it because I didn't have my, my hex, my, um, Allen key set
Because when I got my bag, it had this huge hole in it.
So I just left it there and so then I rode back and tightened my, uh, my cleats and then
I went riding again.
But the chain is too, is too small and it skips when I climb.
So I'm going to have to get a new chain tomorrow.
But, uh, I went on this new part of the trail that I've never done before.
It goes down to the, uh, the road and back.
So, uh, yeah, on one segment I looked at what place I got.
I got like 164th out of like 600 people.
So that's not so bad.
Some downhill.
So, uh, yeah, it was, it's fun, man.
I, I was filming myself with the head cam, but, uh, the helmet cam and it filmed the first
Time I went around and then back.
Like, but they didn't get the second one.
It said like the format was wrong.
Oh yeah.
But then the battery was dead.
So I put these batteries I got from Radio Shack.
Those don't work.
So I'm just using my rechargeable batteries.
It shows that that's working.
So hopefully it'll work a lot.
I have 30 gig a lot of bike cart in there.
This is enough for like, I think it said, did the calculations, right?
Like four days worth of filming.
So that's not a problem.
I can just turn it on the beginning of the ride and just leave it on and then come home and
Get the good bits.
But it was pointing too far down.
Um, I got, I went in the bike shop also to get some like, uh, pump straps to put it
On the helmet.
And there's some, some parts where it was doing all right, but what I'm going to do tomorrow
Is just set it up perfectly, you know, so that I know exactly where I'm looking.
Um, you know, I know what I'm filming and then that'll be good.
So then I'll go up there again.
It's only open during the day though.
It sucks.
I wanted to do some night writing because I have a really good light.
Let's see if Walker Ranch has them, has that.
Be cool to just go on some like single track over and over again all through the night.
So, uh, yeah, tomorrow we're going to go in at three to finish up moving all the stuff
Back into my mom's room.
So until then I'll, yeah, I'll just organize here.
So it's all coming together.
It's exciting.
And mountain biking on the Tesla is so fun.
It's like ultimate mount, like single track.
Like at one point when I was doing well, I was like, felt like the tire was just, you
Know, I could just keep it on the trail and just go, but like, yeah, I'm rusty.
Like I was, uh, you know, almost going completely off the trail a few times and wiggling around.
I was going after this one guy for a while and he was going fast and I was feeling him
That would look cool, but it didn't record that he was dead by then.
Okay.
So I've rambled on enough for the day.
